title: Physical Badge Access to Data Center Containing Cardholder Systems

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 9.4.2 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 9.4.2 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.11.56: Physical Badge Access to Data Center Containing Cardholder Systems.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.56' running on index=physical sourcetype="pacs:door" ‚Äî reader_id, badge_id, result, site, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

spl:
index=physical sourcetype="pacs:door" earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_dc_readers.csv reader_id OUTPUT cde_dc_zone
| where cde_dc_zone="true"
| eval denied=if(match(result,"(?i)deny|reject|unknown"),1,0)
| timechart span=1d sum(denied) as denied_reads, count as total_reads
| eval deny_rate=if(total_reads>0, round(100*denied_reads/total_reads,2), null())
description: Tracks granted and denied door reads for suites housing payment hardware so physical entry to sensitive areas is logged and reviewable for onsite PCI assessments.
value: Tracks granted and denied door reads for suites housing payment hardware so physical entry to sensitive areas is logged and reviewable for onsite PCI assessments.
implementation: (1) Map reader IDs to PCI zones from facility diagrams. (2) De-identify `badge_id` in dashboards if required. (3) Alert on sustained deny spikes (tailgating). (4) Quarterly reviewer export.
visualization: Line (deny_rate), column (denied_reads), table (top denied badges hashed).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We track granted and denied door reads for suites housing payment hardware so physical entry to sensitive areas is logged and reviewable for onsite assessments ‚Äî so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom `sourcetype="pacs:ccure"` or `lenel:access"` CSV/HEC.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=physical` `sourcetype="pacs:door"` ‚Äî `reader_id`, `badge_id`, `result`, `site`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Map reader IDs to PCI zones from facility diagrams. (2) De-identify `badge_id` in dashboards if required. (3) Alert on sustained deny spikes (tailgating). (4) Quarterly reviewer export.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="pacs:door" earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_dc_readers.csv reader_id OUTPUT cde_dc_zone
| where cde_dc_zone="true"
| eval denied=if(match(result,"(?i)deny|reject|unknown"),1,0)
| timechart span=1d sum(denied) as denied_reads, count as total_reads
| eval deny_rate=if(total_reads>0, round(100*denied_reads/total_reads,2), null())
```

Understanding this SPL

**Physical Badge Access to Data Center Containing Cardholder Systems (PCI DSS Req 9.2.1, 9.4.2)** ‚Äî Tracks granted and denied door reads for suites housing payment hardware so physical entry to sensitive areas is logged and reviewable for onsite PCI assessments.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=physical` `sourcetype="pacs:door"` ‚Äî `reader_id`, `badge_id`, `result`, `site`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Custom `sourcetype="pacs:ccure"` or `lenel:access"` CSV/HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: physical; **sourcetype**: pacs:door. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=physical, sourcetype="pacs:door", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where cde_dc_zone="true"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **denied** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets ‚Äî ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **deny_rate** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line (deny_rate), column (denied_reads), table (top denied badges hashed).
